story: Near closing time, I sat alone in a back booth when a shy, tearful man calling himself Jason begged me to check on his girlfriend, who he said had been in the bathroom too long. His plea was a trap: there was no waiting girlfriend, and he meant to ambush me and take me to a cabin in the woods. The bar was quiet, and he kept wiping at his eyes. I started to rise from my seat.
implausible_knowledge: His plea was a trap: there was no waiting girlfriend, and he meant to ambush me and take me to a cabin in the woods.
question: What will the narrator most plausibly do next?
answer_with_knowledge: Back away and alert the bar staff or police instead of following him.
answer_without_knowledge: Go with Jason toward the bathroom area to check on the supposed girlfriend.
